title: Pan-Seared Fish with Minted Peas & Yogurt Tartar
description: Clean, simple pan-seared white fish over grains with buttery minted peas and a yogurt-based tartar sauce with cornichons, capers, and dill.

ingredients.name: white fish fillets (sole, cod, or halibut)

instructions.text: Rinse quinoa or farro and cook according to package directions. Set aside covered to keep warm.
Make the tartar sauce: mix yogurt, cornichons, capers, shallot, dill, and lemon zest and juice in a bowl. Season with salt and pepper. Refrigerate until serving.

Make the minted peas: boil salted water and cook frozen peas about 4 minutes until tender. Drain well, return to pot with butter, and crush lightly with a potato masher. Off heat, stir in lemon zest and mint. Season with salt and pepper. Hold the lemon juice until just before serving.

Pat fish fillets dry and season both sides with salt and pepper. Heat avocado oil in a skillet over medium-high until shimmering. Add fillets and sear without moving, 3–4 minutes until golden. Flip and cook 2–3 minutes more until just opaque through the center.
Stir lemon juice into peas just before serving. Plate the grain, top with minted peas, the seared fish, and a generous spoonful of tartar sauce.

notes:
Sole is most delicate, halibut is meatiest — all work well here. Don't move the fish once it hits the pan; let it release naturally for a clean sear.

The tartar sauce keeps well refrigerated for 3–4 days and doubles as a dip for vegetables.
